items.question: Which condition defines a convex function f?
options: f(t x + (1-t) y) <= t f(x) + (1-t) f(y) for t in [0,1]
items.answer: f(t x + (1-t) y) <= t f(x) + (1-t) f(y) for t in [0,1]
items.explanation: This chord (Jensen) property is the standard definition; the other options are special cases or unrelated.

items.question: For a convex function over a convex domain, a local minimum is:
items.explanation: Convexity prevents suboptimal valleys; any local minimum is globally optimal.

items.question: At a critical point, Hessian eigenvalues [3, -1] imply:
items.explanation: Mixed positive and negative eigenvalues mean indefinite curvature—a saddle.

items.question: Which training problem is convex in its parameters (weights)?
items.explanation: Standard logistic loss is convex in w; ReLU MLPs and most deep models are non-convex in parameters.
